source_jobs.title: Customer Success Agent
source_jobs.description:
About Slash

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over three billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We're backed by some of the best investors in the world including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Stanford University, and the founders of Tinder and Plaid. Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.


We’re growing fast and are looking for sharp, detail-oriented Customer Success Agent to join our team!


The Role:
As a Customer Success Agent, you will play an important role in ensuring our customers have an excellent experience while maintaining the highest standards of compliance with our platform.



What You’ll Do:

 - Handle customer success and support operations, working closely with our product, sales, and engineering teams.

 - Document and automate customer operation processes where possible.

 - Conduct customer support: Serve as a first point of contact for customer questions via phone, email, and dashboard.

 - Troubleshoot account issues, guide customers through onboarding, and resolve inquiries efficiently.

 - Review new customer onboarding applications.

 - Write FAQs and internal playbooks to make our customer experience faster and easier.

 - Managing inbound support tickets and calls with professionalism and empathy.
   

What We’re Looking For:

 - 2+ years in financial services, customer support/success or sales/BDR experience preferred.

 - Strong attention to detail and excellent written/verbal communication skills.

 - Comfortable handling sensitive information and exercising discretion.

 - Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines.

 - Experience with Intercom, Pylon, or similar tools a plus but not required.

 - Familiarity with AML, KYB/KYC, or transaction monitoring preferred but not required (we’ll train you).

 - Ability to work Day Shift - PST or EST hours (9am-6pm)
   

Why Join Slash:

 - Be part of a fast-growing fintech startup disrupting business finance.

 - Work closely with compliance leadership to develop in-demand skills in AML and risk operations.

 - Opportunity to grow into high-impact compliance roles as we scale.

 - On-Site or Remote opportunity!

source_jobs.title: Partnerships Lead – Accounting Firms
source_jobs.description:
Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over ten billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries.

We are looking for a Partnerships Lead to build relationships with accounting firms and financial service providers.



What You'll Do:

 - Develop partnerships with accounting firms and financial advisory practices

 - Position Slash as the preferred banking and treasury platform for their clients

 - Build relationships with partners and firm leadership

 - Drive referrals and onboarding of businesses through accounting firm networks

 - Represent Slash at accounting industry events and conferences

 - Collaborate with product teams to develop financial tools tailored for accountants and their clients



Nice To Have:

 - Strong network within accounting firms or financial advisory practices

 - Experience working with accountants, CFO services, or financial operators

 - Understanding of financial workflows for small and mid-sized businesses

 - Strong partnership-building ability



What’s In It For You:

 - Opportunity to build a major distribution channel for Slash

 - Competitive base salary plus performance incentives ($150k+ OTE)

 - Equity in Slash

 - Work with our team in San Francisco

 - Unlimited PTO, Health, Vision, and Dental coverage

 - Paid Lunches

source_jobs.title: Associate General Counsel, Product and Commercial
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time.

We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate, not generic banking products retrofitted with software. We’re creating a new category of business banking that combines the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and uncompromising security) with industry-specific software, stablecoins, and AI-native workflows.

Our goal is simple: make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Founded in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest-growing fintech companies in the world. We power billions in annual transaction volume across a wide range of industries — from internet-native founders to traditional operators. We’ve raised over $60M from leading investors including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Goodwater Capital, Stanford University, and founders of companies like Tinder and Plaid.

Our team brings together fintech operators from Cash App, Brex, and Finix, alongside founders who have built and scaled their own businesses. We’re headquartered in San Francisco (with another office in New York City) and operate with a strong in-person culture.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring an Associate General Counsel to help build the legal foundation for Slash as we scale. You’ll report to our General Counsel and work closely with the leadership team, including our two founders.

This is a broad, high-leverage role working across the company. You’ll be involved anywhere legal intersects with the business: from shaping new products and helping us navigate an evolving regulatory landscape to reviewing commercial agreements and structuring partnerships.

You’ll act as both a thought partner and an operator. The goal is not just to spot risk, but to help the company move forward with clarity and speed.

You will:

 - Advise on a wide range of legal matters across product, commercial, and regulatory

 - Support the development and launch of new financial products and features

 - Draft, review, and negotiate key agreements (partnerships, vendors, customers, and more)

 - Help structure and execute strategic relationships with banks, fintech partners, and infrastructure providers

 - Partner with compliance, risk, and operations teams to align on regulatory strategy

 - Build internal processes, templates, and frameworks that scale with the company

 - Identify areas of legal or regulatory risk and propose practical paths forward

 - Manage external counsel where needed

You’re expected to be sharp, creative, and independent, to leverage automation and AI tools effectively, and to step in wherever support is needed.

Location: San Francisco or New York City




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of legal experience (law firm, in-house, and/or government)

 - Strong generalist skillset across commercial, regulatory, and/or product-related legal work

 - Experience in fintech, financial services, banking, or high-growth technology companies

 - Excellent judgment and ability to balance legal risk with business objectives

 - Clear and concise communication style

 - Ability to work cross-functionally with product, engineering, and leadership

 - Very high ownership mindset




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience supporting expansion into new consumer products, capabilities, or regulatory frameworks

 - Familiarity with crypto, stablecoins, or AI

 - Prior experience at an early-stage or scaling startup

 - Experience building legal processes from scratch






YOU’LL THRIVE HERE IF

 - You like being a generalist and jumping across different problem areas

 - You’re comfortable making decisions without perfect information

 - You move quickly and communicate clearly

 - You enjoy working closely with founders and operators

 - You want your work to directly shape how the company builds and scales




BENEFITS

 - Competitive base salary ($240K–$300K)

 - Equity in a high-growth c

source_jobs.title: Head of Credit
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time.

We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate, not generic banking products retrofitted with software. We’re creating a new category of business banking that combines the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and uncompromising security) with industry-specific software, stablecoins, and AI-native workflows.

Our goal is simple: make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Founded in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest-growing fintech companies in the world. We power billions in annual transaction volume across a wide range of industries — from internet-native founders to traditional operators. We’ve raised over $60M from leading investors including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Goodwater Capital, Stanford University, and founders of companies like Tinder and Plaid.

Our team brings together fintech operators from Cash App, Brex, and Finix, alongside founders who have built and scaled their own businesses. We’re headquartered in San Francisco (with another office in New York City) and operate with a strong in-person culture.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Head of Credit to own and scale our credit offerings. This person will be responsible for building the credit infrastructure, underwriting policies, partnerships, and team needed to grow originations into the hundreds of millions monthly while maintaining best-in-class loss rates.

This is not a back-office risk role. You'll sit at the center of the business, working closely with growth, product, engineering, and leadership to make credit decisions that directly enable business growth.

You should be deeply engaged with customer needs, have strong judgment under uncertainty, and know what it takes to scale underwriting responsibly.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own end-to-end credit product development, including understanding customer needs, building underwriting models, finding financing, managing launches, and growing the loan book

 - Scale originations for corporate charge cards and other credit offerings to $100M+ in monthly originations while maintaining low default and delinquency rates

 - Design and implement automated credit decisioning systems in partnership with engineering team

 - Build reporting & monitoring systems to provide clear visibility into credit portfolio

 - Lead stress testing, scenario analysis, and reserve methodology calculations to ensure resilience across credit cycles

 - Manage relationships with capital partners, rating agencies, and other potential partners as needed

 - Build, mentor, and scale a high-performing credit and risk team


WHO YOU ARE

 - 10+ years of experience in commercial lending in a credit, risk management, or underwriting role

 - Proven track record of scaling a commercial lending portfolio with low loss rates

 - Deep fluency in credit modeling, scoring, and alternative data; you've built or significantly improved underwriting models directly

 - Experience designing automated credit decisioning pipelines and working cross-functionally with product and engineering

 - Strong understanding of funding structures and their tradeoffs (including warehouse facilities, forward flow arrangements, and securitizations)

 - Ability to move fast without cutting corners; you know how to say yes responsibly and no with conviction

 - Excellent communicator who can translate complex risk concepts for internal and external stakeholders

 - Experience operating in a regulated environment and familiarity with applicable consumer & commercial lending regulations

 - Based in San Francisco or New York City and excited to work in-person 5 days a week


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience underwriting in SMB lending (term loans, lines of credit, revenue-based financing, or merchant cash advances)

 - Background at a top-tier fintech (e.g., Brex, Ramp, Aff

source_jobs.title: Applied AI Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over ten billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We recently raised a $100M Series C led by Ribbit Capital with participation from Khosla Ventures, Goodwater Capital, NEA, and Y Combinator, accelerating our expansion into new markets and products.

Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Slash is building an AI-native financial platform, and we're forming Slash AI Labs, a small, high-leverage team responsible for bringing AI deeply into every surface of the product. We've already shipped Twin, our agent platform powered by a custom orchestration runtime, tool-calling over a structured ontology graph, MCP, and multi-surface delivery across web, Slack, and API. You'll be an early member of this team and helping incubate what comes next. If you want to build production AI systems at a fintech that's growing fast and ships constantly, this is the role.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Ship full-stack AI features end to end, from prompt engineering and agent orchestration to React UI and API integration

 - Build and extend our agent runtime: orchestration, tool execution, MCP servers, graph-based conversation state, and context compaction

 - Design and implement multi-surface agent experiences across web, Slack, and programmatic APIs

 - Build internal AI tooling and platforms, model evaluation frameworks, prompt management, and LLM observability tooling

 - Work directly with Anthropic and OpenAI APIs, the Vercel AI SDK, and our custom orchestration layer to solve challenging problems in tool-calling, long-context management, and multi-step reasoning

 - Improve and scale our AI infrastructure: inference routing, model tiering, prompt caching, and streaming architecture on AWS/EKS



WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO

 - Has shipped full-stack AI products to production, not just prototypes, but systems that serve real users reliably

 - Is proficient in TypeScript or modern web frameworks (React, Next.js, Node.js) with strong backend fundamentals

 - Has hands-on experience with LLM APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI), with a strong grasp on tool-calling, structured output, and streaming

 - Can build and iterate quickly, you're a builder who ships, not someone who over-plans

 - Has experience with one or more of: agent orchestration, MCP, RAG, structured extraction, or model evaluation



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

 - Opportunity for high growth

 - High autonomy + ownership culture

 - Comprehensive health + benefits plan

 - Working out of our downtown San Francisco office space

 - Unlimited PTO

source_jobs.title: Senior Infrastructure/Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over ten billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We recently raised a $100M Series C led by Ribbit Capital with participation from Khosla Ventures, Goodwater Capital, NEA, and Y Combinator, accelerating our expansion into new markets and products.

Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Slash is, at its core, a technology company and is on a mission to build the best engineering team in the world. We’re looking for a Senior Infrastructure/Platform Engineer focused on owning and scaling the foundation of our banking platform as we grow fast.

You’ll be responsible for keeping our systems reliable, secure, and fast — while building the tooling and infra that enables the rest of the team to move quickly without breaking things. That means scaling and working on everything on the cloud (AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes).

You’ll define our infrastructure roadmap, own critical decisions around performance, observability, security, and deployment, and play a key role in enabling Slash to serve billions of dollars in business spend reliably and securely.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Influence and implement the next generation of Slash's database, real-time workflow, and container orchestration infrastructure

 - Scale and evolve our Kubernetes (EKS) platform, CockroachDB clusters, Kafka (MSK), Temporal workflows, and ElastiCache Redis as we grow through hypergrowth

 - Work across engineering to introduce and scale best practices with cloud-native technologies like AWS ALB, WAF, Route 53, OpenSearch, S3, and Vercel

 - Build and maintain abstractions within Terraform and Pulumi to simplify architecture, increase velocity, and push ownership to product teams

 - Improve the speed and reliability of our CI/CD pipeline

 - Find solutions to Slash's toughest scaling, performance, and low-latency problems across a monolithic deployment serving burst financial traffic



WE’RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO

 - 2-3+ years of experience shipping high-quality infrastructure for critical, high-availability systems

 - Production experience in AWS (EKS, ALB, MSK, ElastiCache, S3, WAF, VPC networking)

 - Proficiency in TypeScript or another object-oriented language

 - An ability to think through internal engineering requirements and come up with high-impact ways to quickly unblock teams

 - Deep experience in one or more of the following:
   
   - Large-scale distributed SQL database administration (e.g. CockroachDB, PostgreSQL)
   
   - Real-time workflow and queue systems (e.g. Temporal)
   
   - Container orchestration and service infrastructure (Kubernetes/EKS, load balancing, ingress, service mesh)



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

 - Opportunity for high growth

 - High autonomy + ownership culture

 - Comprehensive health + benefits plan

 - Working out of our downtown San Francisco office space

 - Unlimited PTO

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Growth
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over ten billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We recently raised a $100M Series C led by Ribbit Capital with participation from Khosla Ventures, Goodwater Capital, NEA, and Y Combinator, accelerating our expansion into new markets and products.

Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is a highly technical, high-velocity Software Engineer role on our growth team. This team is our PMF engine and is responsible for making bets and building features to break into new markets. You will build on top of our core platform and ship new features in hours to days, not weeks. Growth engineers at Slash deeply understand the business and work directly with customers every day. You will partner closely with our top AEs to build, iterate, and ship vertically integrated solutions for customers and prospects, then turn those into scalable platform features. You will also be joining a world-class growth org that has scaled to $300M+ in revenue with a team of less than 10. Ultimately, we are looking for people with extreme drive, strong curiosity, and a bias toward action.





WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Deliver end to end software solutions for our customers, understand pain points, scope product specs, and ship solutions that close deals and activate accounts
• Generalize custom work into platform features, take a one-off integration or workflow and turn it into something thousands of Slash customers can use
• Partner closely with Sales and Customer Success across the full lifecycle — from prospect to rollout to expansion




WE'RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO

• Moves fast and defaults to action
• Is highly customer-oriented and excited to work directly with users
• Has strong product + business intuition (cares about outcomes, not just code)
• Brings intensity and hustle in a high-velocity environment
• Has solid technical foundation and can ship end-to-end quickly
• Thrives in ambiguity and takes full ownership



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

 - Opportunity for high growth

 - High autonomy + ownership culture

 - Comprehensive health + benefits plan

 - Working out of our downtown San Francisco office space

 - Unlimited PTO

source_jobs.title: Senior/Staff Software Engineer, Product
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over ten billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We recently raised a $100M Series C led by Ribbit Capital with participation from Khosla Ventures, Goodwater Capital, NEA, and Y Combinator, accelerating our expansion into new markets and products.

Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Slash is, at its core, a technology company and is on a mission to build the best engineering team in the world. We’re looking for a Senior/Staff Software Engineer to help build and evolve our core banking product that powers $10b in transaction volume a year. This role blends infrastructure-level thinking with deep product intuition. You’ll build fault-tolerant, user-facing financial primitives that spans across all customers, and you’ll do it fast, cleanly, and with ownership.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own and ship entire product surfaces end-to-end, from database schema and API design to building the product customers interact with daily

 - Build and scale our core financial infrastructure: payments, transfers, invoicing, bill pay, onboarding, and card management systems that move tens of billions of dollars a year

 - Design data models, write migrations, and build the backend services that power complex multi-step financial operations

 - Work directly with product, design, and compliance to ship features that are correct, compliant, and actually good to use — in financial software, the details are the product

 - Shape engineering standards, contribute to architecture decisions, and talk to customers to figure out what to build next




WE'RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO

 - Has shipped and owned full product surfaces — not just isolated features, but entire systems from data model to deploy

 - Is a strong full-stack engineer: comfortable designing API endpoints, writing a database migration, and building the frontend for a project end to end

 - Has strong product instincts — thinks holistically about UX, API ergonomics, and system correctness, and pushes back when something doesn't feel right

 - Thrives working independently in a small team that ships fast — you're the kind of person who builds product without requiring a full spec

 - Cares about craft: writes clean, well-tested code, thinks about edge cases, and takes pride in building software that is delightful to use

 - Bonus: Has an intuition for financial systems (payments, ledgers, compliance) or a genuine desire to go deep on them




WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

 - Opportunity for high growth

 - High autonomy + ownership culture

 - Comprehensive health + benefits plan

 - Working out of our downtown San Francisco office space

 - Unlimited PTO

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Stablecoin
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over ten billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We recently raised a $100M Series C led by Ribbit Capital with participation from Khosla Ventures, Goodwater Capital, NEA, and Y Combinator, accelerating our expansion into new markets and products.

Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Slash's stablecoin and on-chain infrastructure team builds the rails powering some of the fastest-growing surfaces of the company. We launched stablecoin payments less than a year ago and have already crossed $3B in annualized volume. Our Global USD Account is a USD account for businesses in 130+ countries, backed by USDSL, our own stablecoin issued on Base has become the entry point for thousands of businesses globally that have never had real access to U.S. dollar rails. Our Global Card lets customers spend their stablecoin balance anywhere Visa is accepted. We're hiring a product engineer to build the next chapter of all of this. You'll work on the Global USD Account, our expanded global account offerings, stablecoin payments, and the issuing stack behind the Global Card. If you want to build infrastructure where on-chain rails meet traditional banking and ship it to real businesses moving real money, this is the role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own and ship full product surfaces end-to-end across the Global USD Account, expanded global accounts, stablecoin payments, and our Global Card — from data model to API to the product customers interact with daily

 - Build and extend our core stablecoin infrastructure: on-chain execution, on-ramp and off-ramp orchestration, USDSL mint/redeem flows, and the settlement systems that move billions of dollars a year

 - Build the issuing stack for our Global Card, where stablecoin balances integrate with card rails, handling spend authorization, settlement, and the financial primitives that make a stablecoin-backed card feel as smooth as a traditional debit

 - Expand the Global USD Account into new markets and new use cases, giving more businesses real access to U.S. dollar rails

 - Work directly with customers to figure out what to build next, and partner with compliance and design to ship it correctly




WE'RE LOOKING FOR SOMEONE WHO

 - Has shipped and owned full product surfaces, not just isolated features but entire systems from data model to deploy

 - Is a strong full-stack engineer: comfortable designing API endpoints, writing database migrations, and building the frontend end to end

 - Has strong product instincts and thinks holistically about UX, API ergonomics, and system correctness, and pushes back when something doesn't feel right

 - Is excited to go deep on on-chain systems, stablecoins, and the messy seams between crypto and traditional finance

 - Thrives working independently in a small team that ships fast

 - Cares about craft: writes clean, well-tested code, thinks about edge cases, and takes pride in building software that's delightful to use and correct under load




STRONGLY PREFERRED

 - Comfortable reasoning about on-chain UX and settlement mechanics, including signing, finality, gas, and confirmation tradeoffs

 - Hands-on experience integrating with stablecoin on/off-ramp orchestration APIs, custody providers, or settlement infrastructure

 - Familiarity with cross-border money movement, FX, or treasury systems




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience

source_jobs.title: Crypto Compliance AML Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over three billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We're backed by some of the best investors in the world including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Stanford University, and the founders of Tinder and Plaid. Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.

We’re growing fast and are looking for a sharp, detail-oriented Compliance Analyst to join our team!



ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Crypto Compliance AML Analyst at Slash, you’ll play a key role in building and scaling a compliance program tailored to digital assets. You’ll work cross-functionally with Compliance, Operations, Product, and Engineering to monitor on-chain and off-chain activity, investigate risk, and ensure Slash meets its regulatory obligations in a rapidly evolving crypto landscape.

This role is ideal for someone with prior experience who can operate with autonomy, exercise strong judgment, and contribute to improving compliance systems, tooling, and processes.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Conduct AML and fraud monitoring across fiat and crypto transactions, including deep-dive investigations into complex or high-risk activity

 - Analyze blockchain transactions and wallet behavior to identify suspicious patterns and exposure to illicit activity

 - Perform customer due diligence (CDD) and enhanced due diligence (EDD), including evaluating source of funds/wealth and crypto-specific risk factors

 - Independently investigate potentially suspicious activity and draft clear, well-supported internal escalation reports and/or Unusual Activity Reports (UARs)

 - Support KYC/KYB processes, including onboarding reviews and periodic customer refreshes

 - Monitor direct and indirect blockchain exposure with high-risk and severe-risk categories, including screening wallet addresses and counterparties e

 - Maintain thorough documentation, audit trails, and case records in line with regulatory expectations

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to refine transaction monitoring rules, improve alert quality, and enhance crypto compliance tooling

 - Stay current on evolving crypto regulations, enforcement trends, and typologies (e.g., mixers, sanctions evasion, DeFi exploits)

 


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience in AML, compliance, fraud, or risk operations (fintech, crypto, banking, or payments preferred)

 - Strong understanding of AML frameworks, including transaction monitoring, SAR/UAR processes, KYC/KYB, and sanctions compliance

 - Solid understanding of blockchain fundamentals (wallets, transactions, token flows, DeFi) and common crypto risk typologies

 - Ability to independently investigate and resolve complex cases with sound judgment

 - Strong analytical skills with attention to detail across both on-chain and off-chain data

 - Clear, concise written communication, especially in investigative documentation

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced startup environment with evolving regulatory expectations

 - High integrity and sound judgment when handling sensitive financial and blockchain data



NICE TO HAVE (BUT NOT REQUIRED):

 - Experience with blockchain analytics tools (e.g., Chainalysis, TRM Labs, Elliptic)

 - ACAMS certification
   
   


WHY JOIN SLASH:

 - Be part of a fast-growing fintech startup disrupting business finance.

 - Work closely with compliance leadership to develop in-demand skills in AML and risk operations.

 - Opportunity to

source_jobs.title: Head of Brand
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time.

We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate, not generic banking products retrofitted with software. We’re creating a new category of business banking that combines the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and uncompromising security) with industry-specific software, stablecoins, and AI-native workflows.

Our goal is simple: make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Founded in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest-growing fintech companies in the world. We power billions in annual transaction volume across a wide range of industries — from internet-native founders to traditional operators. We’ve raised over $60M from leading investors including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Goodwater Capital, Stanford University, and founders of companies like Tinder and Plaid.

Our team brings together fintech operators from Cash App, Brex, and Finix, alongside founders who have built and scaled their own businesses. We’re headquartered in San Francisco (with another office in New York City) and operate with a strong in-person culture.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Head of Brand Marketing to define and scale how Slash shows up in the world.

This is a foundational role. You’ll own top-of-funnel brand awareness and be responsible for turning Slash into a category-defining fintech brand with real cultural presence.

You’ll work closely with the founders and leadership team to translate our product vision into something that resonates far beyond fintech. The goal is not just to run campaigns, but to build a brand people recognize, remember, and talk about.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy, creative, and execution. You’ll operate as both a builder and an owner, defining the playbook while actively deploying it.




YOU WILL:

 - Own top-of-funnel brand awareness across all major channels, including video, creators, audio, and out-of-home

 - Design and execute a multi-channel brand strategy spanning streaming/TV, podcasts, newsletters, social media, sponsorships, and more

 - Own budget allocation across channels, balancing cultural impact with efficiency

 - Translate complex product and company narratives into simple, memorable, mass-market storytelling

 - Create high-impact brand moments: campaigns, activations, and ideas that break through and drive outsized attention

 - Build and manage paid brand infrastructure, including agency relationships, partnerships, and media buying

 - Define how we measure success across brand, from awareness and brand lift to long-term growth signals

 - Partner closely with content and organic growth teams to ensure a unified narrative across paid and owned channels

 - Build the brand function from the ground up, including systems and playbooks

 - Have the opportunity to grow and lead a world-class brand team as the company continues to scale
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - 8+ years of experience in brand marketing, growth, or related roles

 - Experience building or scaling a brand at a high-growth company

 - Experience owning and deploying large brand budgets

 - Deep familiarity with channels like video/streaming, creators, and integrated campaigns

 - Strong storytelling ability: you can take complex ideas and make them simple and compelling

 - Clear judgment on where to spend and what will actually break through

 - Ability to operate both strategically and tactically

 - Comfort working in environments where outcomes are not perfectly measurable

 - High ownership mindset and ability to work directly with founders
   
   


NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience in fintech, financial services, or adjacent industries

 - Background working with creator ecosystems or cultural partnerships

 - Experience managing agencies or large external vendor relationships

 - Prior experience building

source_jobs.title: Head of YouTube
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time.

We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate, not generic banking products retrofitted with software. We’re creating a new category of business banking that combines the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and uncompromising security) with industry-specific software, stablecoins, and AI-native workflows.

Our goal is simple: make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Founded in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest-growing fintech companies in the world. We power billions in annual transaction volume across a wide range of industries — from internet-native founders to traditional operators. We’ve raised over $60M from leading investors including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Goodwater Capital, Stanford University, and founders of companies like Tinder and Plaid.

Our team brings together fintech operators from Cash App, Brex, and Finix, alongside founders who have built and scaled their own businesses. We’re headquartered in San Francisco (with another office in New York City) and operate with a strong in-person culture.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Head of YouTube to build and own a category-defining content engine at Slash.

This is not a traditional social media role. You’ll own YouTube end-to-end – from strategy and content to distribution and performance – with the goal of making Slash one of the most trusted and widely consumed financial brands on the internet.

You’ll work directly with the founder and leadership team to translate complex financial concepts into content that millions of people want to watch. YouTube is the anchor, but your mandate extends across all content channels, helping define how Slash shows up across the internet.

This is a zero-to-one role with full ownership. You’ll define the playbook, build the system, and scale it.




YOU WILL:

 - Own YouTube growth end-to-end, including content strategy, packaging, and distribution

 - Drive performance across titles, thumbnails, retention, and algorithm dynamics

 - Build and manage a consistent content calendar and publishing cadence

 - Translate complex financial products into engaging, accessible storytelling that educates and converts

 - Build and manage a creator ecosystem, including sourcing, onboarding, and working with creators and production partners

 - Own production end-to-end, including allocating resources across formats, creators, and experiments

 - Expand content strategy across platforms like Instagram, TikTok, and emerging channels

 - Ensure a cohesive narrative across all owned content channels

 - Define and build Slash’s content identity from the ground up

 - Have the opportunity to build and lead a world-class content team as the company scales




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR:

 - Deep expertise in YouTube, including strong intuition for algorithm, thumbnails, titles, and retention

 - Proven experience growing a YouTube channel (your own or for a creator) to meaningful scale

 - Strong creative instincts paired with analytical rigor

 - Experience building and managing creator ecosystems

 - Operator mindset: you can execute, not just strategize

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and building from scratch

 - Clear communication and ability to work directly with founders

 - High ownership mindset




NICE TO HAVE:

 - Experience at media or high-growth tech companies is a plus

 - Experience in fintech, financial services, or adjacent industries

 - Background working with creator ecosystems or cultural partnerships

 - Experience managing agencies or large external vendor relationships

 - Prior experience building a brand function from scratch




YOU’LL THRIVE HERE IF:

 - You have strong taste and instincts for what will resonate culturally

 - You’re as comfortable shipping campaigns as you are defining strategy

 - You like

source_jobs.title: General Interest Application
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time.

We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate, not generic banking products retrofitted with software. We’re creating a new category of business banking that combines the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and uncompromising security) with industry-specific software, stablecoins, and AI-native workflows.

Our goal is simple: make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Founded in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest-growing fintech companies in the world. We power over $30 billion in annualized payment volume and have raised more than $160 million, including a $100 million Series C led by Ribbit Capital, at a $1.4 billion valuation.

Our team brings together fintech operators from Cash App, Brex, and Finix, alongside founders who have built and scaled their own businesses. We’re headquartered in San Francisco (with another office in New York City) and operate with a strong in-person culture.





ABOUT THE ROLE

We're always looking for exceptional people to join Slash.

This application is for candidates who are excited about what we're building but don't see an open role that perfectly matches their background today. We're growing quickly across a variety of functions and are interested in meeting talented operators, builders, and problem-solvers who want to help shape the future of business banking.

At Slash, titles matter less than impact. The people who thrive here are highly adaptable, move quickly, and are eager to take ownership of important problems. As we continue to scale, opportunities emerge across many areas of the business, and we're constantly looking for individuals who can help us build, execute, and push the company forward.

Whether your experience is in operations, business development, partnerships, customer success, recruiting, finance, marketing, strategy, legal, or something else entirely, we'd love to hear from you.





YOU WILL

 - Help solve high-priority problems across the business

 - Take ownership of projects and drive them from idea to execution

 - Work closely with teammates across multiple functions

 - Identify opportunities to improve systems, processes, and customer experiences

 - Use technology, automation, and AI to increase leverage and efficiency

 - Operate with urgency while maintaining a high bar for quality

 - Contribute wherever support is needed as the company grows




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong track record of achievement in your field

 - Exceptional problem-solving and communication skills

 - Ability to learn quickly and operate in unfamiliar environments

 - High ownership mindset and willingness to take initiative

 - Strong execution skills and attention to detail

 - Comfort working in a fast-moving, ambiguous environment

 - Desire to work closely with a high-performing team and ambitious founders

 - Ability to leverage AI and automation tools effectively
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience at a startup or other high-growth environment

 - Experience building processes or systems from scratch

 - Evidence of exceptional initiative

 - Background in fintech, financial services, software, or related industries

 - Experience working across multiple functions or disciplines
   
   


YOU'LL THRIVE HERE IF

 - You enjoy being a generalist and wearing many hats

 - You seek ownership rather than waiting for direction

 - You move quickly and communicate clearly

 - You're energized by ambitious goals and high standards

 - You enjoy figuring things out when the path isn't obvious

 - You care more about impact than job titles

 - You want to help build something category-defining from the ground up
   




BENEFITS

 - Competitive base salary

 - Equity in a high-growth company

 - Best-in-class health, dental, and vision insurance

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Daily in-office

source_jobs.title: Senior Video Editor
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is a financial technology company building the modern money stack for businesses. We help founders and operators run their entire financial workflow in one place, and we are growing fast off the back of a brand that punches well above its size. Marketing here is creative-first. We invest in original, high-craft content rather than scaling headcount, and video sits at the center of that bet.

We are hiring a Senior Video Editor to own the craft of our most important work: customer case studies, sit-down interviews, and product demos that look and feel like they came out of a top-tier studio.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This is not a social-clips role. We are looking for a genuinely talented editor who has shipped larger, higher-quality projects and can carry a piece from raw footage to a polished, broadcast-grade final cut. The ideal person is both a shooter and an editor, comfortable behind a camera on a shoot day and equally at home in the timeline.

You will work directly with our marketing leadership on flagship content, with real creative ownership and a high bar for taste, pacing, and finish.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Edit customer case studies, sit-down interviews, and product demos into polished, story-driven films.

 - Own post-production end to end: assembly, story structure, pacing, sound design, and color.

 - Shoot when needed, on location and in studio, as a hybrid shooter-editor.

 - Color grade footage to a clean, cinematic standard inside DaVinci Resolve.

 - Collaborate on creative direction and bring your own ideas, not just execute a brief.
   



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - A strong portfolio of larger, high-quality projects. We want to see range across long-form and narrative work, not only social edits.

 - Real editing talent with excellent instincts for pacing, story, and audio.

 - Experience in Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve, and a willingness to edit in DaVinci Resolve (we standardize on it for color and team collaboration).

 - The ability to take a project from footage to finish with minimal hand-holding.

 - Comfortable being a shooter as well as an editor. This is a strong plus, not a hard requirement.
   
   


NICE TO HAVE

 - Hands-on experience shooting with Sony FX3.

 - Color grading in S-Log3 / S-Gamut3.Cine inside DaVinci Resolve.

 - Motion graphics and animation chops (helpful, not required).

 - 3D ability in Blender for custom renders. This would be a real differentiator for us, though it is not necessary.
   
   


LOCATION

San Francisco is strongly preferred. We have a tight-knit in-office culture and being in person makes collaboration and shoot days far easier. Remote is possible for an exceptional candidate.




COMPENSATION

$70,000–$100,000, depending on experience and whether you bring shooting ability alongside editing. This role is cash compensation.




HOW TO APPLY

Apply through our Ashby posting and include a link to your portfolio. Show us a clear scope of the work you have done: the kind of projects, your specific role on each, and the pieces you are proudest of. We care most about high-quality, larger projects, so lead with those.

source_jobs.title: Product Manager, Enterprise Finance
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is reimagining the future of business banking, helping companies prepare their financial workflows for the fundamental shifts driven by technologies like AI and crypto.

We’re building an elevated financial OS that combines the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and uncompromising security) with future-proof capabilities like stablecoins, tailored financial software, and agentic finance.

Our core tenet is a relentless focus on our customers. We serve a wide spectrum of businesses: resourceful internet entrepreneurs, traditional SMB owners, and ambitious founders building at the bleeding edge of technology. We move quickly on customer feedback—building scalable, generalizable products as well as industry-specific software that strengthens our moat within each vertical we serve.

Founded in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest-growing fintech companies in the world. We power over $10B in annual transactions across a diverse range of industries. To date, we’ve raised over $60M in venture funding, from leading investors like Goodwater Capital, Menlo Ventures, NEA, and Y Combinator.

Our team is deeply experienced yet dynamic, bringing together leaders from fintech companies like Cash App, Mercury, and Finix, alongside self-starters who have built and exited 7–8 figure businesses from their dorm rooms. Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, California.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Enterprise Finance Product Manager, you will become an expert on the CFO/controller/bookkeeper persona, extracting insights from existing customers and future adopters.

You’ll develop a clear point of view on how software can improve finance workflows in the status quo, while rethinking the role those same workflows may play in an increasingly agent-driven future.

Working closely with a small, high-caliber team of engineers, you’ll drive high-quality features with speed and taste. At the same time, you will collaborate directly with Sales to build a user-informed roadmap and unblock high-value deals.

You will fully own the design, customer adoption, and revenue of our Enterprise Finance product suite, including:

 - Accounts Receivable: From invoice creation and delivery to payment collection and reconciliation. This includes building robust invoicing tools, real-time AR aging and performance reporting, and automated collections workflows.

 - Accounts Payable: Bill ingestion and processing, scheduled and recurring payments, approval workflows, vendor onboarding and management, and integrated procurement capabilities.

 - Expense Management: Empower finance leaders to define and enforce expense policies across large organizations. Includes receipt capture, multi-layer approval workflows, and a flexible low-code policy engine built for opinionated CFOs.

 - Accounting Integrations: Enable business owners and finance teams to categorize and manage transactions through intuitive, AI-driven workflows that accelerate time-to-close while maintaining accuracy and integrity with ERP systems such as QuickBooks, Sage, NetSuite, Xero, and more.

We believe exceptional products are built through real-world collaboration. This role is in-person at our San Francisco office, five days a week.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Fully own the roadmap, strategy, and product metrics for Slash’s Enterprise Finance product suite

 - Distill insights from customers, conferences, and industry research, and proactively share them to inform product strategy and team decision-making

 - Drive product adoption and usage in close partnership with Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success

 - Stay ahead of finance industry trends and advancements in AI to inform long-term product strategy

 - Build and manage relationships with external vendors and ecosystem partners as needed
   
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of product management experience

 - Exceptionally high agency: you see what needs to be done and execute without waiting for directio

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant to the CEO
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over three billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We're backed by some of the best investors in the world including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Stanford University, and the founders of Tinder and Plaid. Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.

We’re growing fast and are looking for a highly organized, proactive Executive Assistant to support our CEO!



ABOUT THE ROLE

As Executive Assistant to the CEO, you'll be a trusted partner responsible for keeping our CEO organized, focused, and operating at maximum effectiveness. You'll manage a fast-moving schedule, coordinate travel, handle day-to-day logistics, and ensure nothing falls through the cracks. This is a high-impact role that requires exceptional attention to detail, strong judgment, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced startup environment.

You'll work closely with the CEO and collaborate across the company to help drive operational excellence while maintaining the highest level of professionalism and discretion.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Manage a busy, constantly evolving calendar, proactively prioritizing meetings, resolving scheduling conflicts, and protecting focus time

 - Coordinate domestic and international travel, including flights, hotels, ground transportation, and itineraries

 - Monitor the CEO's inbox for scheduling requests and ensure timely responses and follow-up

 - Handle a wide range of administrative requests, including appointments, reservations, and other personal and professional logistics

 - Plan and coordinate company events such as offsites, team dinners, all-hands meetings, and executive gatherings

 - Support office management and day-to-day operational logistics as needed

 - Anticipate the CEO's needs, proactively solving problems before they arise

 - Serve as a reliable point of coordination across internal teams and external partners

 - Provide occasional administrative support to other team members, including coordinating travel and schedules for conferences, customer meetings, and other business events
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 5+ years of experience supporting a CEO or other C-suite executive in a fast-paced environment

 - Proven ability to manage a demanding, constantly changing calendar with sound judgment and attention to detail

 - Strong experience coordinating complex travel logistics and itineraries

 - Exceptional organizational and time management skills, with the ability to juggle multiple priorities simultaneously

 - High level of discretion and professionalism when handling confidential information

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Calm under pressure and resourceful when navigating last-minute changes

 - Flexible and available to accommodate occasional off-hours requests related to travel or urgent business needs

 - Highly proactive with a strong sense of ownership and follow-through
   


NICE TO HAVE (BUT NOT REQUIRED)

 - Experience supporting a founder or executive at a high-growth startup

 - Experience planning company events or executive offsites

 - Familiarity with Google Workspace, Slack, Notion, and modern productivity tools
   


WHY JOIN SLASH

 - Be part of one of the fastest-growing fintech startups redefining business banking.

 - Work directly alongside the CEO and gain exposure to high-impact strategic initiatives.

 - Play a critical role in help

source_jobs.title: Onboarding Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SLASH

Slash is building the future of business banking, one industry at a time. We believe businesses deserve financial infrastructure tailored to how they actually operate. That's why we're creating a new category of business banking. We combine the reliability of traditional banking (high yields, competitive rewards, and comprehensive security) with industry-specific features that make businesses more efficient, more competitive, and more profitable.

Started in 2021, Slash is one of the fastest growing fintechs in the world and we power over three billion dollars a year in business purchasing across numerous industries. We're backed by some of the best investors in the world including Menlo Ventures, NEA, Y Combinator, Stanford University, and the founders of Tinder and Plaid. Slash is headquartered in San Francisco, and has a strong in-person culture.

We’re growing fast and are looking for a sharp, detail-oriented Compliance Analyst to join our team!




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Onboarding Analyst at Slash, you'll play a key role in ensuring new customers are onboarded safely, efficiently, and in compliance with regulatory requirements. You'll work closely with Compliance, Operations, Sales, and Product teams to review new customer applications, conduct due diligence, and help deliver a seamless onboarding experience while maintaining strong AML controls.

This role is ideal for someone early in their compliance career who wants hands-on exposure, real ownership, and the opportunity to grow alongside a high-impact fintech company.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Review new customer onboarding applications for completeness, accuracy, and compliance with internal and bank partner requirements.

 - Verify customer identities and business documentation as part of KYC/KYB onboarding.

 - Perform Customer Due Diligence (CDD) and Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD).

 - Conduct sanctions, PEP, and adverse media screening during the onboarding process.

 - Assess AML and onboarding risk and escalate higher-risk applications when appropriate.

 - Communicate with customers and internal teams to obtain additional documentation or resolve onboarding issues.

 - Maintain accurate onboarding records, audit trails, and compliance documentation.
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - High agency and a strong sense of ownership. You proactively identify problems, take initiative, and follow through without needing constant direction.

 - Strong attention to detail and analytical skills, with the ability to make thoughtful, risk-based decisions.

 - Clear written and verbal communication skills.

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving startup environment where processes continue to improve over time.

 - Exposure to AML, customer onboarding, compliance, fraud, or risk operations is a plus, but not required. We'll teach you the technical aspects — you bring the drive to learn and execute.





WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU:

 - Opportunity for high growth

 - High autonomy + ownership culture

 - Comprehensive health + benefits plan

 - Unlimited PTO

notes: Fintech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, high growth rate
